question_text: A metal cube has a mass of 270 g and a volume of 30 cm³. A second metal cube made of a different material has a mass of 400 g and a volume of 50 cm³. Which cube is denser, and by how much? (Give your answer in g/cm³.)
correct_answer: The first cube is denser. It has a density of 9 g/cm³ compared to 8 g/cm³ for the second cube, so it is 1 g/cm³ denser.
explanation: First cube: density = 270 ÷ 30 = 9 g/cm³. Second cube: density = 400 ÷ 50 = 8 g/cm³. The difference is 9 − 8 = 1 g/cm³, making the first cube denser.
